prompt: The overloaded method resolves to the wrong version. What is the bug?
choices: Java resolves overloaded methods at runtime based on the actual object type
Java resolves overloaded methods at compile time based on the declared (static) type; o is declared as Object so print(Object) is called
The print(String) method shadows print(Object) and should always be preferred
Passing a String to an Object parameter causes implicit boxing
explanation: Overload resolution in Java is a compile-time decision based on the static type of the argument. Since o is declared as Object, the compiler selects print(Object). At runtime the actual type is String, but overloads (unlike overrides) are not polymorphic. Fix: cast to String or use instanceof before calling.
